title: Cardiaca marrubiastrum
intro: Cardiaca marrubiastrum, commonly known as Marrubium, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Marrubium include its potential to treat various gastrointestinal issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Marrubium include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and diterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Marrubium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
